svg.title: call me maybe — project learning roadmap
svg.desc: Five phases from setup to constrained decoding, each with concepts and resources

g.text: Phase 1 — Python project structure
pyproject.toml · uv · Makefile · flake8 · mypy · pydantic · type hints · docstrings
Resources: docs.astral.sh/uv · peps.python.org/pep-0257 · mypy.readthedocs.io
pydantic-docs.helpmanual.io · flake8.pycqa.org
Phase 2 — JSON input/output + argument parsing
Read/write JSON files · argparse for CLI flags · error handling (try/except)
Understand the two input files: function_calling_tests.json, functions_definition.json
Resources: docs.python.org/3/library/json.html
docs.python.org/3/library/argparse.html
Phase 3 — LLM basics + the SDK
What tokens are · what logits are · what input_ids are
Use the llm_sdk: encode() → get_logits_from_input_ids() → argmax → next token
Get the vocabulary JSON and understand token→string mapping
Resources: jalammar.github.io/illustrated-gpt2 (visual, beginner-friendly)
huggingface.co/learn/nlp-course/chapter2 (tokenization section, read-only)
Phase 4 — Function selection with the LLM
Build a prompt: describe available functions + the user's natural language request
Ask the LLM to pick the correct function name by constrained decoding
At each step: mask invalid tokens (set logit = -inf), pick from valid ones only
Valid tokens at position 0 of function name = only tokens that start a valid fn name
Resources: arxiv.org/abs/2307.09702 (outlines paper — read intro + section 2)
blog.dottxt.co/say-what-you-mean.html (constrained decoding, plain English)
Phase 5 — Argument extraction (constrained JSON generation)
Once function is chosen, generate its arguments token-by-token
Track JSON state machine: are we in a string? number? after a colon?
Only allow tokens that keep the JSON valid at each position
Enforce schema: e.g. field "a" must be a number → block string tokens there
Resources: json.org (state diagram for JSON grammar)
github.com/guidance-ai/guidance (inspiration, don't import it)

svg.text: Build phases 1–2 first — they're pure Python with no ML. Once the skeleton works,
layer in phases 3–5 one at a time. Test each phase independently.
